steps.title: Recognize the condition for infinitely many solutions
steps.content: An equation has infinitely many solutions when both sides are identical expressions — true for all values of $x$. The given equation is $4x+12=\frac{a(x+3)}{2}$. Notice the left side factors: $4x + 12 = 4(x+3)$, so rewrite it as $$4(x+3) = \frac{a(x+3)}{2}$$

steps.title: Solve for a by matching coefficients
steps.content: Multiply both sides by 2 to clear the fraction: $$8(x+3) = a(x+3)$$ For this to be true for all values of $x$, the coefficients of $(x+3)$ on both sides must be equal. Therefore $a = 8$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
